findings.locus: §2.2 Per-source-class calibration table — Environment row 'Realization routes to: Built — the inherited-confidence derivation ruling's aging basis' and 'Control-type: Per-policy reliability-weighting at retrieval'
findings.claim: The Environment control is described as reliability-weighting realized 'at retrieval', but the named realizing authority (DDR-004) fixes the aging derivation as computed at Evidence capture and frozen — DDR-004 §1/§3 state confidence is derived-at-creation and 'never recomputed at read'. The record claims a mechanism its own cited realization contradicts: the control is capture-time-frozen, not at-retrieval.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 (Environment row) vs DDR-004 §1/§3 (derived-at-creation, frozen, never recomputed at read)

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Ruling D (findings db343ac9 + 9d7e8220), plus DDR-004 §1/§3 (capture-time-frozen aging, never recomputed at read) and §6 (two-tier calibration governance)

findings.locus: §2.2 Per-source-class calibration table — Environment row 'Realization routes to: Built' and §5.3 Risks 'Empirical floor'
findings.claim: The Environment control is labeled 'Built' in the calibration table, yet §5.3 concedes calibration constants are held contested and unvalidated at zero traffic, and the realizing ruling (DDR-004) holds base/τ as contested tunables. The 'Built' status claim is not what the record's own risk section and cited realization support — the form is built, the control-as-calibrated is not — and the table asserts a completeness the body retracts.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 (Environment 'Built') vs §5.3 Risks (constants contested/unvalidated, n=0) and DDR-004 §2/§6 (contested tunables)

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Ruling D (relabel §2.2 Environment status to 'form built (DDR-004); constants contested/unvalidated (n=0)')

findings.locus: §2.3 Un-promotion authority — 'governed by the same per-write, EA-gated, human-accountable control as promotion itself'
findings.claim: §2.3 claims un-promotion is governed by the same control as promotion, but the built retraction instance it cites (graph-schema ruling §5, retraction-gated invariant #21) traces to *an* approving decision, not the *governing* one, and therefore does not supply promotion's governing-verdict guarantee (#15). §5.3 concedes exactly this gap. The 'same control' claim overstates fidelity relative to what the cited retraction shape delivers.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.3 ('same control') vs §5.3 Risks (executed-retraction re-decision detection gap; #21 traces to approving not governing decision) and DDR-002 §7 #15/#21

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings, Ruling F (qualify §2.3 'same control' to match the §5.3 concession; authority homed, governing-verdict detection analogue named as follow-up — DDR-002 §7 #21 traces to *an* approving decision not the *governing*)

findings.locus: §2.2 Operational distillation row — 'Realization routes to: The forthcoming feedback-loop governance design; the detection-promotion service design' and §5.2 Constraints Imposed
findings.claim: The record decides a per-distinct-lesson human review control for operational distillation and states it is realized through a candidate-`ObservedPattern` shape (§5.2). DDR-002 §2.3 leaves the distillation-write checkpoint posture 'explicitly un-decided' and defines no candidate-`ObservedPattern` class; §2.2/§5.2 present a forthcoming, additive downstream shape as if it were the settled realization route. The decision's declared realization artifact does not yet exist in the substrate it points to.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2/§5.2 (candidate-ObservedPattern realization) vs DDR-002 §2.3 (distillation checkpoint explicitly un-decided; no candidate-ObservedPattern class defined)

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings, Ruling E (candidate-ObservedPattern shape forthcoming/additive) + author-decision-record SKILL body-integrity earned-tense rule

findings.locus: §7 Cross-References — 'ADR-004 (file-driven ingestion, forthcoming)' and 'DDR-003 (Feedback Loop Governance, forthcoming)'
findings.claim: Forward references to unauthored downstream records are stated with concrete identifiers (ADR-004, DDR-003) rather than subject-name. The record's own body elsewhere routes to 'the forthcoming ingestion decision record' and 'the forthcoming feedback-loop governance design' by subject-name; the §7 concrete-identifier form commits an identifier binding that the referenced records have not yet earned, and the ADR-004 number collides with the ADR-numbering already in play. This is a scope-declaration defect: the record names a dependency by an identifier it cannot yet own.

cited_authority.ref: author-decision-record SKILL, Body integrity — 'A forward reference in the body is subject-named only … Concrete identifiers bind only at the downstream record's landing'

findings.authority_locus: Ruling G (ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings) + author-decision-record SKILL body-integrity forward-reference rule

findings.locus: §2.2 Promotion row — 'Realization routes to: Built — the graph-schema ruling's PromotionDecision gate and its promoted-origin invariant'
findings.claim: The promotion control's realization citation is stated as a subject-named reference ('the graph-schema ruling') rather than resolving to whether ADR-008 depends on it as a named artifact-plus-locus. The record depends on DDR-002 §5/§7 #15 for the built promotion instance but the §2.2 table cell names it only descriptively, leaving the dependency's citable artifact+locus implicit where §7 must carry it explicitly.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 (Promotion 'graph-schema ruling') vs §7 Cross-References (DDR-002 §5/§7 #15 named) — dependency-naming consistency

findings.authority_locus: Ruling B (RBT-59/ADR-008 operator rulings): 'fix the Promotion-class authority citation to the durable chain — ADR-001 §2.5 + DDR-002 §5 / §7 #15'

findings.locus: §2 Decision + §2.6 Governance boundary — the invariant/control-type/policy altitude split
findings.claim: Checked whether the record claims to decide more than it declares: §2 fixes the invariant, accountability, and control-type per class, and §2.6 explicitly disclaims the remedy boundary, thresholds, cadence, retention, and reviewer-role assignments, routing them to the forthcoming feedback-loop governance design. Attacked for a smuggled policy decision riding under the principle; the boundary held — no threshold, cadence, or remedy ruling is decided in the body, consistent with the deliberation record's ratified ADR↔DDR-003 boundary (Item 5).

cited_authority.ref: RBT-59 deliberation record Item 5 — 'ADR owns invariant + accountability + control-type + lifecycle; remedy-boundary and all governance policy → DDR-003; ADR does not rule the remedy-boundary'

findings.locus: §2.1 scope — 'Reasoning-Graph capture is not ground truth … outside this record's scope'
findings.claim: Checked whether the record's title/summary scope (mutation of enterprise ground truth) matches its decision content: §2.1 explicitly excludes Reasoning-Graph capture as captured judgment governed by ADR-001 §2.2/ADR-002 §2.6, and confines the invariant to KG authoritative ground truth. Attacked for scope creep into RG capture governance; the exclusion held and is consistent with the cited authorities — the decision content stays within the declared ground-truth-mutation scope.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.1 scope exclusion vs ADR-001 §2.2 (reasoning-capture invariant) and ADR-002 §2.6 (author/executor separation)

findings.locus: §2.2 table, Environment row — Control-type: 'Per-policy reliability-weighting at retrieval; no per-write gate' / Realization routes to: 'Built — the inherited-confidence derivation ruling's aging basis'
findings.claim: The ADR states the Environment control is reliability-weighting 'at retrieval'. The ratified authority it cites for this control, DDR-004, fixes the mechanism as capture-time-frozen, not at retrieval: DDR-004 §3 states confidence 'is computed once at capture and frozen onto Evidence.confidence; it is never recomputed at read', and DDR-004 §1's Environment branch derives base × exp(−Δt/τ) at Evidence capture. The design's stated control-type deviates from the mechanism in its own cited realization authority.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §3 (frozen: 'computed once at capture and frozen ... never recomputed at read'); DDR-004 §1 (branch (ii) Environment aging derived at capture)

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings, Ruling D (drop 'reliability-weighting at retrieval'; state capture-time-frozen aging + DDR-004 §6 governance; relabel status), citing DDR-004 §1/§3

findings.locus: §2.2 table, Environment row — Realization: 'Built — the inherited-confidence derivation ruling's aging basis'
findings.claim: The Environment control is asserted 'Built', but the cited authority substantiates only that the control's form is built while its constants are contested and unvalidated: DDR-004 §2 flags base (0.9) and τ (90 days) as *(contested)* tunables with 'Empirical floor: zero capture traffic to date; these values are unvalidated', and DDR-004 §6 states 'no calibration is possible until the platform runs'. The unqualified 'Built' label is unsubstantiated against its own cited realization record; the ADR's own §5.3 Risk concedes the constants are provisional, contradicting the flat 'Built' claim in §2.2.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 Environment 'Built' vs ADR-008 §5.3 'Empirical floor ... calibration constants ... held contested'; DDR-004 §2/§6 (constants unvalidated, n=0)

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Ruling D: relabel §2.2 'Built' to 'form built (DDR-004); constants contested/unvalidated (n=0)'

findings.locus: §2.2 (Environment control: 'no per-write gate') vs §2.5 (cross-class precedence: 'the mutation is blocked and surfaced')
findings.claim: §2.2 fixes the Environment class as carrying no per-write gate on the observation stream, while §2.5 states that any mutation conflicting with another class's control 'withholds and escalates' — 'the mutation is blocked and surfaced'. As written, a routine Environment observation that structurally collides with an EA-set governance scope is subject to a per-write block, which the design does not reconcile against §2.2's 'no per-write gate' commitment. The record is silent on whether the §2.5 withhold is a mechanical gateway-level conflict detection (firing only on collision) or a human gate, leaving the two sections in unresolved tension at the point where an Environment write meets a promoted-knowledge scope.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 Environment 'no per-write gate' in conflict with ADR-008 §2.5 'the mutation is blocked and surfaced'

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Ruling C (§2.2 vs §2.5 cross-class precedence reconciliation)

findings.locus: §2.2 table, Operational distillation row — Realization routes to: 'The forthcoming feedback-loop governance design; the detection-promotion service design' / §5.2 Constraints: 'realized through a candidate-ObservedPattern shape excluded from ground-truth traversal until approved — a downstream governance design and an additive schema shape'
findings.claim: The design presents the candidate-ObservedPattern shape as the realization of the distillation control, but its cited schema authority does not contain this shape and explicitly leaves the question open: DDR-002 §2.3 records the distillation-write checkpoint posture as 'explicitly un-decided', routing it to the forthcoming KG-entry-governance ADR. The candidate-ObservedPattern is therefore a forthcoming/additive shape, not an existing schema constraint; §5.2 states it in present-realization voice ('realized through a candidate-ObservedPattern shape excluded from ground-truth traversal until approved') without marking it as not-yet-existing, and the promotion-boundary CandidatePromotion class (DDR-002 §5) does not cover derived ObservedPattern distillation.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 §2.3 (distillation-write checkpoint 'explicitly un-decided', routed to forthcoming KG-entry-governance ADR); DDR-002 §5 (CandidatePromotion scope)

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008 operator ruling E (run-025 dispositions): candidate-ObservedPattern shape stated as forthcoming/additive, not present schema constraint

findings.locus: §2.3 Un-promotion authority — 'governed by the same per-write, EA-gated, human-accountable control as promotion itself'
findings.claim: §2.3 claims un-promotion is governed by 'the same ... control as promotion itself' and names the built retraction shape as its instance, but the cited built instance does not supply promotion's governing-verdict guarantee: DDR-002 §7 #21 traces a RETRACTS edge to *an* approving PromotionDecision, whereas the promotion control's #15 keys off the *governing* (latest-decided_at) approving edge, and DDR-002 §2.4 explicitly names the executed-retraction re-decision detection analogue as unruled and routed to DDR-003. The 'same control' claim is therefore not substantiated by the instance the section itself cites; the design's own §5.3 Risk ('Executed-retraction re-decision detection ... no standing detection analogue yet') concedes the gap the §2.3 claim overstates.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 §7 #21 (retraction traces to *an* approving decision) vs DDR-002 §7 #15 (promotion keyed to *governing* edge); DDR-002 §2.4 (executed-retraction re-decision analogue deferred)

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Ruling F (§2.3 'same control' qualification): authority principle holds; qualify to match §5.3 concession, governing-verdict analogue = named follow-up (DDR-002 §7 / DDR-003)

findings.locus: §7 Cross-References — 'ADR-004 (file-driven ingestion, forthcoming)' and 'DDR-003 (Feedback Loop Governance, forthcoming)'
findings.claim: The record cites forthcoming, unauthored records by concrete document identifier (ADR-004, DDR-003). The governing authoring discipline requires forward references to unauthored records to be subject-named only, with concrete identifiers binding at the downstream record's landing; the ADR-004 identifier additionally collides in namespace with the existing DDR-004 the record already depends on, creating a resolution hazard. This is a cross-reference-integrity deviation from the body-integrity forward-reference rule.

cited_authority.ref: author-decision-record SKILL, body integrity: 'a forward reference in the body is subject-named only ... The record stays standalone-buildable'

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008 operator rulings Ruling G (run-025/026 dispositions) + author-decision-record SKILL body-integrity forward-reference rule

findings.locus: §7 Cross-References, DDR-003 entry — 'the obligation to resolve DDR-003's pointer to this record's concrete document identifier lives in DDR-003's definition of done'
findings.claim: The Supersedes field reads 'None — establishes new platform principle', consistent with a new-principle ADR; however the record simultaneously asserts it homes and supersedes authority that ADR-001 §2.5 does not reach without an explicit 'Relationship to ADR-001 §2.5' statement recording generalization/subsumption. The §2 and §2.2 prose assert §2.5 'is cited, not superseded' but the position is stated in passing rather than as a discrete recorded relationship, leaving a reader to reconstruct the subsumption from scattered clauses.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 Promotion bullet ('§2.5 is cited, not superseded') and §2.3 ('ADR-001 §2.5 authorizes promotion only') — subsumption relationship asserted in passing, not recorded as a discrete cross-reference resolution

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Ruling B — mandates an explicit 'Relationship to ADR-001 §2.5' statement recording generalization + subsumption + un-promotion extension

findings.locus: §2.4 Withdrawal posture — 'Provenance survives mutation' vs DDR-002 §4 Evidence pinning
findings.claim: Attacked the provenance-survival commitment for consistency with the ratified schema's retention mechanics. It holds for promoted facts: DDR-002 §4 freezes Evidence.fact_summary + source_node_version, SOURCED_FROM is rebind:pinned (never re-pointed on supersession), and the ProvenanceSummary is materialized at-promotion (DDR-002 §7 #20). The ADR also correctly and explicitly discloses the residual gap in §5.3 ('decision-consumed evidence ... could age out') rather than overclaiming — the commitment is scoped to what the schema substantiates.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 §4 (Evidence version-pinning, rebind:pinned SOURCED_FROM), §7 #20 (at-promotion ProvenanceSummary completeness); ADR-008 §5.3 (retention-gap disclosure)

findings.locus: §2.5 Cross-class precedence — 'withholds and escalates ... fail-safe direction is to withhold, not admit' vs SDD-001 §3.5.2 SCOPE_CONFLICT
findings.claim: Attacked the cross-class precedence principle for whether its named interim instance actually exists and behaves as claimed. It holds: SDD-001 §3.5.2 defines the SCOPE_CONFLICT block — an ingested successor colliding with an EA-set conditional scope is blocked and surfaced as a governance-significant event, never silently admitted, with remediation routed through the promotion boundary — matching the ADR's withhold-not-admit direction and its escalation-to-human-resolution claim.

cited_authority.ref: SDD-001 §3.5.2 (SCOPE_CONFLICT cross-origin block, disclosed governance-significant event); DDR-002 Named Gaps ('surface-to-EA as a scope-conflict, never silent auto-composition')

findings.locus: §1 Context / §2.6 Governance boundary — dependency on the forthcoming feedback-loop governance design (DDR-003) and the forthcoming ingestion decision record
findings.claim: ADR-008 is stated to end an indecision that multiple accepted records depend on, yet its own operative controls are only realized by records that do not yet exist: the operational-distillation control routes to 'the forthcoming feedback-loop governance design,' the ingestion control to 'the forthcoming ingestion decision record,' and §2.6 concedes the remedy boundary, criteria, thresholds, cadence, retention, reviewer-role assignments, and multi-condition composition are all owned downstream. This ADR ratifies a control-type per class while the mechanism that would make three of the four classes operative is unwritten — the posture question is whether an authority-homing record should stand now when the records that instantiate it are unauthored and, per the substrate, one of them (DDR-003) is itself blocked on this record. The timing of standing this record ahead of the records it depends on for realization deserves scrutiny.

cited_authority.ref: RBT-59-ADR-008-deliberation-record Item 5: 'DDR-003 is a downstream consumer citing this ADR as authority; the ADR never lists DDR-003 as an upstream it depends on' — vs. ADR-008 §1 'The forthcoming feedback-loop governance design is blocked on this record' and §2.2 realization-routes column naming forthcoming records as the realization home for two of four classes

findings.authority_locus: RBT-59 deliberation record Item 5 (ADR fixes invariant + accountability + control-type; DDR-003 downstream consumer, never an upstream dependency) + Item 2 altitude line + Ruling E (forthcoming candidate-shape) + Ruling G (subject-named forthcoming refs)

findings.locus: §2.3 Un-promotion authority — 'governed by the same per-write, EA-gated, human-accountable control as promotion itself'
findings.claim: §2.3 asserts un-promotion is governed by 'the same' control as promotion, but the built instance it names does not deliver the same guarantee: DDR-002 §7 #21 (the retraction-gated invariant) traces to *an* approving decision, whereas the promotion invariant #15 traces to the *governing* (latest-decided_at) approving edge. §5.3 of this same ADR concedes exactly this asymmetry ('a re-decided executed retraction has no standing detection analogue yet'). The §2.3 'same control' claim therefore overstates the reversibility guarantee actually homed — a hard-to-reverse authoritative-ground-truth mutation (un-promotion) is committed to a control posture whose detection analogue is a named gap, and the record's own §5.3 rebuts the §2.3 phrasing.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.3 ('same per-write, EA-gated, human-accountable control as promotion itself') in conflict with ADR-008 §5.3 ('the retraction invariant traces to an approving decision, not the governing one, so a re-decided executed retraction has no standing detection analogue yet')

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings, Ruling F (§2.3 'same control' qualification)

findings.locus: §2.2 Per-source-class calibration table — Environment control-type 'Per-policy reliability-weighting at retrieval'
findings.claim: The Environment control is described as reliability-weighting 'at retrieval,' but the ratified substrate mechanism (DDR-004 §1/§3) derives the aging confidence at Evidence *capture* and freezes it — 'never recomputed at read.' An ADR fixing a control-type per class against a mechanism it mischaracterizes on the capture/retrieval axis pulls against the ratified DDR-004 posture it depends on for this class's realization; the calibration this record ratifies rests on a description its own upstream authority contradicts.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-004 §1/§3 — inherited confidence 'derived at Evidence capture and frozen', 'never recomputed at read' — vs. ADR-008 §2.2 Environment row 'Per-policy reliability-weighting at retrieval' and §2.2 Environment bullet

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Ruling D — drop 'reliability-weighting at retrieval'; state capture-time-frozen aging (DDR-004 §1/§3) + §6 governance; relabel §2.2 status

findings.locus: §2.2 realization-routes column and §7 Cross-References — 'ADR-004 (file-driven ingestion, forthcoming)'
findings.claim: The record cites a forthcoming ingestion decision record as 'ADR-004,' but ADR-004 is not an ingestion record: the substrate defines DDR-004 as the accepted inherited-confidence derivation record and the ADR ladder ADR-003–007 as reserved by standing tickets (triage-001 Item 1), with ADR-004's slot not assigned to ingestion. Committing a concrete forward identifier to an unauthored record — when that identifier collides with an existing accepted record's neighbor and the numbering is not yet allocated — is a premature commitment that binds the platform to a document-identity decision that has not been made. The authoring discipline requires forthcoming records be named by subject only until they land.

cited_authority.ref: author-decision-record SKILL body-integrity rule: 'A forward reference in the body is subject-named only ... Concrete identifiers bind only at the downstream record's landing' — vs. ADR-008 §7 'ADR-004 (file-driven ingestion, forthcoming)' and §2.2 'the forthcoming ingestion decision record'

findings.authority_locus: RBT-59/ADR-008 operator Ruling G + author-decision-record SKILL forward-reference body-integrity rule (subject-name only for unauthored records)

findings.locus: §2.4 Withdrawal and expiry posture — 'Provenance survives mutation' commitment vs. §5.3 retention-gap risk
findings.claim: §2.4 commits as an invariant that 'the traceable rationale of a decision that consumed a ground-truth fact must survive that fact's later mutation,' answerable by traversal to the fact as it stood when consumed. But §5.3 concedes the frozen-provenance survival guarantee is scoped to *promoted* facts only; a decision that consumed a fact without promoting it relies on Reasoning-Graph evidence under bounded retention that 'could age out the graph-native trace.' The record commits a general provenance-survival principle whose realizing mechanism it admits does not yet reach the general case — an over-broad commitment relative to the reversibility the substrate actually supports, deserving its own resolution before the principle is stated unqualified.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.4 ('must remain answerable by traversal to the fact as it stood when it was consumed') in conflict with ADR-008 §5.3 ('The frozen-provenance survival guarantee is currently scoped to promoted facts; ... could age out the graph-native trace')

findings.authority_locus: RBT-59 deliberation record Item 3 (provenance-survives-mutation, promotion-scoped caveat: 'ADR commits the survival requirement; extending the mechanism → named DDR-002 + DDR-003 follow-up') + Standing to-dos #2(a); mirrored by ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Carried follow-ups

findings.locus: §2.1 accountability invariant scope — exclusion of Reasoning-Graph capture from ground-truth mutation governance
findings.claim: Attacked the scope boundary for pulling against prior ratified decisions: whether folding all four mutation paths under one accountability invariant re-opens or contradicts ADR-001's reasoning-capture invariant or ADR-002's write-authority separation. The boundary holds — §2.1 explicitly scopes to KG authoritative ground truth and excludes RG capture (routing it to ADR-001 §2.2 / ADR-002 §2.6), and §2.1 preserves ADR-002 §2.6's author/executor separation by naming who is accountable without relocating authorship onto the gateway. It integrates with, rather than pulls against, the ratified reasoning-capture and system-of-record commitments.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-001 §2.2 (reasoning-capture invariant) and ADR-002 §2.6 (author/executor/authorizer separation) — ADR-008 §2.1 scopes to ground truth, excludes RG capture, and preserves the gateway-as-executor-never-author rule

findings.locus: §2.5 Cross-class precedence — withhold-and-escalate fail-safe direction
findings.claim: Attacked the cross-class precedence rule as a possibly-premature commitment lacking a homed instance. It holds: the withhold-not-admit fail-safe is grounded in a built interim instance (the knowledge-service cross-origin scope-conflict block, SDD-001 §3.5.2) that the record correctly cites as the instance this principle authorizes, and the richer disposition is proportionately deferred to governance policy (§2.6). Reversibility is proportionate — the interim instance is n=0-costless per the substrate, and the principle does not foreclose the downstream resolution disposition.

cited_authority.ref: SDD-001 §3.5.2 (SCOPE_CONFLICT block, mechanical, disclosed) — ADR-008 §2.5 names it as the interim instance this principle authorizes; richer disposition routed to §2.6

findings.locus: §2.2 Per-source-class calibration table — Environment row
findings.claim: The Environment control is stated as 'Per-policy reliability-**weighting** at retrieval' and its realization routes to 'the inherited-confidence derivation ruling's aging basis'. But the ruling it invokes fixes a capture-time-frozen mechanism, not a retrieval-time one: DDR-004 §1/§3 derives Environment confidence as base × exp(−Δt/τ) at Evidence capture and freezes it, explicitly 'never recomputed at read'. The ADR's own §2.2 Environment bullet then says the control 'is a per-policy reliability weighting... at retrieval' — the phrase 'at retrieval' contradicts the frozen-at-capture semantic of the cited realization.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 (Environment control-type: 'reliability-**weighting** at retrieval') in conflict with DDR-004 §1/§3 (aging confidence 'derived at Evidence capture and frozen... never recomputed at read')

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Ruling D (drop 'reliability-weighting at retrieval'; state capture-time-frozen aging per DDR-004 §1/§3 + §6 governance; relabel 'form built; constants contested/unvalidated')

findings.locus: §2.2 Per-source-class calibration table — Environment 'Realization routes to: Built' column
findings.claim: The Environment control is marked 'Built — the inherited-confidence derivation ruling's aging basis', asserting the control is realized. But DDR-004 states only the derivation *form* is built (ACCEPTED) while its constants (base, τ, and the DeploymentEnvironment flat base) are held contested and unvalidated at n=0 (DDR-004 §2 'Empirical floor: zero capture traffic to date; these values are unvalidated'; §6 'no calibration is possible until the platform runs'). An unqualified 'Built' status overstates what the cited authority supplies — the form is built; the calibrated control is not.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 Environment row ('Built — the inherited-confidence derivation ruling's aging basis') in conflict with DDR-004 §2 ('zero capture traffic to date; these values are unvalidated') and §6 ('no calibration is possible until the platform runs')

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025 Ruling D (relabel §2.2 Environment 'Built' to 'form built (DDR-004); constants contested/unvalidated (n=0)') + DDR-004 §2/§6

findings.locus: §2.3 Un-promotion authority
findings.claim: §2.3 states un-promotion is 'governed by the same per-write, EA-gated, human-accountable control as promotion itself' and that the built retraction shape 'is the instance of this authority.' But the built retraction shape does not supply the same control as promotion: DDR-002 §7 #21 (retraction-gated) traces to *an* approving decision, whereas the promotion control (§7 #15) keys off the *governing* approving edge (latest decided_at). §5.3 of this same ADR concedes exactly this — 'the retraction invariant traces to an approving decision, not the governing one, so a re-decided executed retraction has no standing detection analogue yet.' The §2.3 'same control' claim is thus internally inconsistent with §5.3's admission within the same document.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.3 ('governed by the same per-write, EA-gated, human-accountable control as promotion itself'; 'the built retraction shape... is the instance of this authority') in conflict with ADR-008 §5.3 ('the retraction invariant traces to an approving decision, not the governing one') and DDR-002 §7 #21 vs #15

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings, Ruling F (finding ef3f60e2, §2.3): qualify '§2.3 same control' to match the §5.3 concession — authority homed, governing-verdict detection analogue named as follow-up (DDR-002 §7 / DDR-003)

findings.locus: §5.2 Constraints Imposed — Operational distillation constraint
findings.claim: §5.2 states the distillation review is 'realized through a candidate-`ObservedPattern` shape excluded from ground-truth traversal until approved — a downstream governance design and an additive schema shape,' and §2.2's Operational row routes realization to 'the detection-promotion service design.' Read against the substrate, the candidate-ObservedPattern shape does not exist in the ratified schema: DDR-002 §2.3 records the distillation-write checkpoint posture as 'explicitly un-decided,' and the promotion-boundary CandidatePromotion class (DDR-002 §5) covers promotion/retraction only, not distillation candidacy. The §5.2 text names it as a concrete additive schema shape without marking that no such shape is yet ratified — a present-vs-forthcoming tense hazard against the substrate.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §5.2 ('a candidate-`ObservedPattern` shape excluded from ground-truth traversal until approved') in conflict with DDR-002 §2.3 (distillation-write checkpoint posture 'explicitly un-decided') and DDR-002 §5 (CandidatePromotion covers promotion/retraction only)

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Ruling E (finding 3f8adee8, §2.2/§5.2) — state the candidate-ObservedPattern shape as forthcoming/additive, not a present schema constraint

findings.locus: §7 Cross-References — 'ADR-004 (file-driven ingestion, forthcoming)' and 'DDR-003 (Feedback Loop Governance, forthcoming)'
findings.claim: §7 references two unauthored records by concrete identifier — 'ADR-004 (file-driven ingestion, forthcoming)' and 'DDR-003 (Feedback Loop Governance, forthcoming)'. The author-decision-record body-integrity rule requires forward references to unauthored records be subject-named only, never by concrete identifier ('A forward reference in the body is subject-named only... Concrete identifiers bind only at the downstream record's landing'). ADR-002 §4.6 additionally shows 'ADR-004' is not established as the file-driven-ingestion slot in the ratified corpus, making the numbered reference an unresolvable/ambiguous hinge to a stranger reader.

cited_authority.ref: author-decision-record SKILL body-integrity: 'A forward reference in the body is subject-named only — genus plus what's unsettled... Concrete identifiers bind only at the downstream record's landing'

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Ruling G (and author-decision-record SKILL body-integrity forward-reference rule): replace numbered forthcoming references with subject-name forms in §7

findings.locus: §2.5 Cross-class precedence vs §2.2 Environment ('no per-write gate')
findings.claim: §2.2 commits that Environment mutation carries 'no per-write gate' and ages by construction, while §2.5 commits that any cross-class conflict 'withholds and escalates to human resolution' with 'observational or ingested reality never silently takes precedence over an EA-set governance scope.' A reader can take §2.5 as imposing a per-write conflict check on the observation stream that §2.2 declares ungated. The text does not state, within the ADR, that the §2.5 withhold is a gateway-mechanical collision detector firing only on structural collision (not a per-write human gate on the routine stream), leaving the two sections in apparent tension on the Environment class.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.2 ('Per-policy reliability-weighting at retrieval; **no** per-write gate') in apparent conflict with ADR-008 §2.5 ('it withholds and escalates to human resolution; it does not silently win')

findings.authority_locus: ADR-008-run-025-operator-rulings Ruling C (cross-class precedence reconciliation: gateway-mechanical withhold on structural collision, human accountability at resolution, routine observation ungated; instance SDD-001 §3.5.2 SCOPE_CONFLICT)

findings.locus: §2.1 scope exclusion of Reasoning-Graph capture
findings.claim: Checked the claim that Reasoning-Graph capture is out of this ADR's scope ('Reasoning-Graph capture is not ground truth — it is captured judgment, governed by the reasoning-capture invariant and its write-authority assignment (ADR-001 §2.2; ADR-002 §2.6)') against the cited authorities. ADR-001 §2.2 does own the reasoning-capture invariant and ADR-002 §2.6 does assign RG write authority (ASA authors ReasoningProgress; AOE owns ReasoningSession lifecycle); DDR-002 §4 confirms RG nodes carry no KG plane label and are not ground truth. The scope boundary and its citations hold.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.1 scope exclusion vs ADR-001 §2.2 (reasoning-capture invariant) + ADR-002 §2.6 (RG write authority) + DDR-002 §4 (RG carries no plane label) — consistent

findings.locus: §2.1 gateway executor-not-author claim
findings.claim: Attacked the claim that 'the sole-owner graph gateway is the executor of every mutation and is never the authoring authority for it (ADR-002 §2.6)' by checking it against the cited authority and its realizations. ADR-002 §2.6 states the general author/executor/authorizer separation ('The sole-owner gateway (§2.5) is the sole executor... never itself the authoring authority'); SDD-001 §1/§2.1 confirms knowledge-service 'is the sole executor of every authoritative write and the author of none'; DDR-001 Decision.7 and its feedback-loop write-authority treatment carry the same. The claim held against every citation.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-008 §2.1 (gateway executes, never authors) vs ADR-002 §2.6 (author/executor/authorizer separation) + SDD-001 §1/§2.1 (sole executor, author of none) — consistent
